scored_jobs.justification: O candidato possui um bom background em gestão de produtos e programas, com experiência em PO, PM e TPM. No entanto, a vaga de Lead Product Manager exige mais senioridade, com um nível de responsabilidade e escopo mais amplo do que o candidato possui atualmente. Embora o candidato tenha experiência relevante no setor de tecnologia e com abordagens ágeis, a falta de experiência direta na disrupção do mercado imobiliário e no relacionamento entre corretores e financiadores representa um gap de domínio. Portanto, o candidato possui um perfil adequado, mas com alguns riscos que precisarão ser mitigados, resultando em um score de 60.
scored_jobs.main_gap: Seniority gap - o candidato possui menos experiência de gestão de produtos e programas do que o esperado para uma posição de liderança.

eliminated_jobs.title: Product Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
About Lively

Lively set out to raise the bar on benefit solutions, because we believe no one should have to sacrifice personal wellness for financial wellness. While traditional benefits focus more on transactions and less on the humans using them, Lively harnesses user-centric design and innovative technology to deliver an effortless experience for employers and account holders alike. Our modern HSA is consistently top-rated, and we have since expanded our offerings to provide a full suite of other benefits and services for companies, consumers, and financial institutions.

Lively is a remote-first company, headquartered in San Francisco with employees across the US. Come join us and help make getting the value out of your benefits as simple as it should be.

ABOUT LIVELY 

Lively is a leading health savings and benefits platform built for today's modern workforce. We  make it simple for employers, brokers, and employees to maximize the value of HSAs, FSAs,  HRAs, and other benefits accounts. Our platform combines intuitive technology with expert guidance â and we're now building the next generation of AI-powered benefits experiences that  transform how employers administer and employees engage with their benefits. 

We move fast, think big, and build products that have a direct impact on people's financial  health. This is an opportunity to shape products used by thousands of employers and millions  of employees across the United States. 

THE ROLE 

We are seeking an experienced and strategic Senior Product Manager to join our growing team.  In this role, you'll lead product strategy and delivery across Lively's B2B and B2C product  surfaces â from the employer dashboard and broker portal to the employee-facing app and AI powered agent experiences. You'll work at the intersection of healthcare benefits, financial  technology, and applied AI, sha

eliminated_jobs.title: Retouching Project Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Position Summary

The Creative Production Manager oversees retouching and post-production projects from kickoff through final delivery, ensuring all work is completed on time, on budget, and at the highest visual standard. This role serves as the primary day-to-day lead for assigned accounts and projects, balancing client expectations, creative quality, timelines, and internal resources.

The Creative Production Manager partners closely with clients, account teams, retouchers, prepress, and leadership to manage high-volume, deadline-driven workstreams. Success in this role requires strong organizational skills, excellent communication, a sharp creative eye, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities

Project Ownership & Production Management

  • Own all aspects of assigned projects from kickoff through final delivery
  • Manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining accuracy, organization, and quality
  • Establish and maintain project schedules, timelines, milestones, and delivery expectations
  • Oversee day-to-day workflow to ensure deadlines are met and deliverables remain on track
  • Work closely with offshore vendors to maintain quality, consistency, and project schedules, including identifying issues, communicating feedback, and ensuring revisions are completed when necessary.
  • Monitor project scope, flag potential risks, and proactively propose solutions
  • Maintain strict adherence to approved project budgets and escalate concerns when needed

Creative Review & Quality Control

  • Review imagery from a creative and technical perspective to ensure all work meets client expectations and internal quality standards
  • Act as the client’s advocate throughout the retouching process, ensuring imagery aligns with creative direction, campaign cohesion, and project briefs
  • Provide clear, actionable feedback to retouchers and prepress teams to improve image quality and consistency
  • Lead quality control efforts throughout the project lifecycle, serving as the final set of eyes before images are delivered to clients
  • Ensure there are no technical, visual, or file-related issues prior to delivery

Client Communication & Account Partnership

  • Serve as the day-to-day liaison for assigned clients and projects
  • Lead project-related communication including schedules, file deliveries, creative feedback, timelines, and solutions to production challenges
  • Partner closely with Account teams to align on client expectations, project scope, budgets, and delivery timelines
  • Build strong client relationships through professionalism, responsiveness, and attention to detail
  • Participate in client meetings, creative reviews, and production calls as needed

Workflow Coordination & Team Collaboration

  • Coordinate workflow across retouching, prepress, production, and account teams to ensure efficient execution
  • Partner with the Production Lead and Director of Retouching & Post to establish project workflows, staffing plans, and resource allocation
  • Track project status and maintain accurate records of files, markups, approvals, schedules, and communications
  • Attend and lead production meetings, review sessions, and internal project discussions
  • Contribute to a collaborative, team-oriented environment within the department and across cross-functional teams

Estimating, Reporting & Resource Management

  • Create project estimates in partnership with Account teams and leadership
  • Track time spent on images and projects to evaluate resource usage, efficiency, and profitability
  • Produce reports and participate in post-mortem discussions to identify opportunities for workflow improvement
  • Maintain organized project documentation including files, markups, emails, hard drives, and reference materials

Professionalism & Representation

  • Demonstrate professionalism and sound judgment in all client and internal interactions
  • Represent the company positively in meetings, presentations, and public or private events
  • Make decisions in the best interest of the company, clients, and project teams
  • Support a positive, solutions-oriented work environment

Success in This Role Looks Like

  • Projects are delivered on time, on budget, and at a consistently high quality level

  • Clients feel informed, supported, and confident throughout the production process

  • Internal teams have clear direction, organized workflows, and strong communication

  • Imagery is cohesive, visually strong, and aligned with creative expectations

  • Project challenges are identified early and resolved proactively

  • Accurate project tracking, reporting, and documentation are maintained across all accounts

  • 5–7 years of experience in a high-volume production, post-production, or creative environment

  • Experience in photography, beauty, fashion, retail, or related industries strongly preferred

  • Strong understanding of photography, image production, and retouching workflows

  • Working knowledge of Adobe Photoshop, Bridge, Excel, InDesign, and file transfer platforms such as FTP, Dropbox, and WeTransfer

  • Excellent visual judgment and strong color perception

  • Strong time management, communication, and organizational skills

  • Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining attention to detail and creative quality

The anticipated salary range for this role is $70,000 - $90,000 annually. Ranges are based on various factors including the labor market, job type, internal equity, and budget. Exact offers are calibrated by work location, individual candidate experience, and skills relative to the defined job requirements.

This is a hybrid role requiring flexibility to work in-office and remotely. Evening or weekend work may occasionally be required during major projects.

We offer a competitive benefits package including major medical, dental and vision insurance, FSA, HSA, and employee discounts.

Industrial Color is a division of CoCreativ, a family of integrated creative production companies. Industrial Color partners with top brands, agencies, and creative professionals providing a full range of essential production services. For over 30 years, we’ve led the industry through a culture of innovation, bringing state-of-the-art digital production and post-production solutions to our clients.

Our headquarters is based in Tribeca, NY, with offices in Los Angeles and studios in Brooklyn and Los Angeles. Our clients include: L’Oreal, Publicis, Procter & Gamble, Tommy Hilfiger, Absolut Vodka, Nike, Estee Lauder, Amazon…
